жҲ‘е•Ҹе•ҸйЎҢпјҢдҪ ең°зӯ”пјҢжӯЎиҝҺд»»дҪ•жңүиҲҲи¶ЈиҖғж©ҹеё«жҲ–дҝӮзҙ”зІ№жү“зҷјжҷӮй–“еҳ…дәәпјҒ

Q1. What is volumetric efficiency and what factors affect the volumetric efficiency of the cylinder?

The ratio of intake air and fuel inside the combustion chamber(?)
Mass of gas, fuel used, valve timing, volumeteic displacement(?)
жұӮжҢҮе°ҺжғіеҒҡж©ҹеё«пјҢжҲ‘жңғиҷӣеҝғеӯёзҝ’жһ¶


еҠ жІ№пјҒ

Ratio of intake air and fuelеҗҢvolumetric efficiencyдҝӮ2еҖӢе””еҗҢеҳ…жҰӮеҝө

Ratio of intake air and fuelдҝӮжҢҮзҮғж–ҷиҲҮз©әж°Јзҡ„жҜ”дҫӢпјҢиҖҢзҗҶи«–дёҠдҫҶиӘӘпјҢ1:15пјҲ1 fuel 15 airпјүжҳҜжңҖжӯЈзўәзҡ„жҜ”дҫӢпјҲchemically correctпјү

Volumetric efficiencyжҳҜжҢҮ the total number of gas moleculesпјҲair and fuel жңғд»Ҙж°Јж…ӢеҪўејҸиў«йҖҒеҫҖcylinderпјүsuccessfully induced into the cylinder, ж„ҲеӨҡfuel air mixtureжҲҗеҠҹиў«йҖҒеҫҖcylinderж„Ҹе‘іи‘—ж„Ҳй«ҳзҡ„volumetric efficiency.

иҖҢиӢҘжһңиҰҒеҪұйҹҝvolumetric efficiencyе°ұд»ЈиЎЁиҰҒжёӣе°‘/еўһеҠ the total number of gas molecules being successfully induced into the cylinder, жҲ‘еҳ…зӯ”жЎҲжңғдҝӮ1.Ambient Air Density 2.Throttle Position 3.Engine RPM 4.Supercharger

1.Ambient Air Density and temperatureпјҢж„Ҳй«ҳжҷӮair particlesж„Ҳз–Ҹ(high altitude=low air density)пјҢиҮӘиҮӘз„¶з„¶жңғжёӣдҪҺе·Ұvolumetric efficiency,temperatureеҗҢжЁЈйҒ“зҗҶ(high temperature=low air density)

2. Throttle PositionпјҢthrottle butterflyй–Ӣеҫ—и¶іеӨ пјҢair particlesијғжҳ“иў«еҗёе…Ҙ,жүҖд»ҘеҸҜд»ҘеўһеҠ volumetric effieciency

3. RPMпјҢRPMеҫҲй«ҳжҷӮпјҢд»ЈиЎЁcombustionзҡ„йҖҹеәҰжңғеҝ«е·ҰпјҢд»ЈиЎЁintroduction stroke,compress stroke,power stroke, exhaust stroke д№Ӣй–“зҡ„йҖҹеәҰеҝ«е·ҰпјҢи®ҠзӣёжёӣзҹӯдәҶи®“particlesйҖІе…Ҙcylinderзҡ„жҷӮй–“пјҢжүҖд»ҘжңғжёӣдҪҺvolumetric efficiency

4. Supercharger,е‘ўй»һжҲ‘е””зҹҘдҪ еӯёе·ҰжңӘпјҢдҪҶsuperchargerеӯҳеңЁзҡ„зӣ®зҡ„е°ұжҳҜзӮәдәҶincrease the volume of airmass being used in the cylinder,жүҖд»Ҙз•¶з„¶superchargerеҸҜд»ҘеўһеҠ volumetric efficiencyдәҶпјҒ

жҲ‘е•Ҹе•ҸйЎҢпјҢдҪ ең°зӯ”пјҢжӯЎиҝҺд»»дҪ•жңүиҲҲи¶ЈиҖғж©ҹеё«жҲ–дҝӮзҙ”зІ№жү“зҷјжҷӮй–“еҳ…дәәпјҒ

жңүе””жҳҺзҷҪеҳ…ең°ж–№жӯЎиҝҺеҶҚе•ҸпјҒеҸӘиҰҒдҝӮеӯёиЎ“жҖ§иіӘжҲ‘еҘҪжЁӮж„Ҹи§Јзӯ”пјҒ

Q2. When you are in a climb, you suspect fuel detonation, what would you do?

a. Lean mixture, reduce throttle, increase airspeed.
b. Richen mixture, reduce throttle, decrease airspeed
c. Richen mixture, reduce throttle, open cowl flap
d. Richen mixture, increase throttle, apply boost pump, open cowl flap

Q2. When you are in a climb, you suspect fuel detonation, what would you do?

a. Lean mixture, reduce throttle, increase airspeed.
b. Richen mixture, reduce throttle, decrease airspeed
c. Richen mixture, reduce throttle, open cowl flap
d. Richen mixture, increase throttle, apply boost pump, open cowl flap


C?

Richen mixture -> richer mixture: higher fuel to air ratio -> more cool fuel injected to the combustion chamber -> lower the temp -> less prone to another detonation

Reduce throttle -> less fuel and air mixture introduced for combustion -> not further stressing the piston & allowing piston recovery

Open cowl flap -> more air are introduced for dissipating excess heat by convection after detonation

жңүйҢҜи«ӢжҢҮжӯЈ

Open cowl flap -> more air are introduced for dissipating excess heat by convection after detonation

Q3.
Explain simply, what is a maximum continuous power[MCP]?

In what condition will we use a power setting beyond MCP?


е‘ўеҖӢжҲ‘е””дҝӮеҘҪиЁҳеҫ—
жҲ‘еҚ°иұЎдёӯ MCP дҝӮеҗҢжҷӮй–“жңүй—ң
Take off/climbing ж—ўжҷӮеҖҷеҸҜд»Ҙз”Ёж—ўе…¶дёӯдёҖеҖӢpower setting
иҖҢеҖӢpower setting еҸҜд»ҘйҖЈзәҢдҪҝз”Ёxx еҲҶйҗҳпјҲ60?пјү

Condition: Go around


дҝӮ зҡ„зўәеҗҢжҷӮй–“жңүй—ң

By definition, it is the maximum power you can use continuously without damaging the engine.

Power setting beyond MCP will usually be used in a take-off, usually with a time limit, specified in the Pilot Operation Handbook.

a. Because it helps to cool engine by carrying heat to the oil cooler and also seals the place between cylinder walls

b. It protects the metal components of the engine from carbon monoxide, nitrogen and helium, it also cools the engine by carrying the heat to the oil cooler

c. It protects the cylinder wall of the combustion chamber during the power stroke and also reduces the exhaust gas temperature

d. It reduces the weight of fuel tank during a climb

Q5. Explain simply why do we need an oil cooler bypass?


е””иӯҳ
жҲ‘дј°дҝӮhigher cooling efficiency? Lower risk of overheating oil temp, 9 дј°


е””дҝӮ

Oil cooler bypass is for low temperature oil to flow.

Since there is no need to cool the oil if the oil is not heated (eg. after start-up/ after a long descent), the bypass provides a short distance for the cold oil to flow, without going through the oil cooler.

Q6.
You have just started up an aircraft, what do you expect to see?

a. Low oil pressure, low oil temperature
b. High oil pressure, low oil temperature
c. Low oil pressure, fluctuating fuel flow
d. High oil pressure, fluctuating cylinder head temperature


A
Before engine start: oil pump not functional yet, as engine drivea the oil pump, oil pressure needs time to build up -> low oil pressure
Aircraft outdoor overnight -> cold before engine is up and ready

Incorrect

Oil pressure indicates the resistance to flow, if you have a high oil pressure, it means you have a high resistance to flow, in simple word, you have sticky oil and it doesn't want to flow.

When you start-up an aircraft, the oil is cold, it has low temperature.

Also, it is not able to flow freely, the force being used to push the oil around is large, therefore it will be indicating a high oil pressure.

Also, it is not able to flow freely, the force being used to push the oil around is large, therefore it will be indicating a high oil pressure.

Q7.

Give 2 advantages and disadvantages for using carburettor fuel system


Adv:
Simpler structure -> lower maintenance burden and complexity


Disadv:
Carburettor: icing of vapours
High G maneuvers for aerobatic a/c: will suffer shortage of fuel as fuel feeding is gravity dependent

Q7.

Give 2 advantages and disadvantages for using carburettor fuel system


Adv:
Simpler structure -> lower maintenance burden and complexity


Disadv:
Carburettor: icing of vapours

Dis:

Q8.
One cause of high oil temperature and low oil pressure could be

a. Very low oil level in the sump
b. The oil's viscosity is too high for the engine type
c. The oil sump is overfilled
d. The oil cooler bypass is not working


A
Too few oil for cooling: high temp
Low quantity of oil: low oil pressure

зҡ®е·ІжӯЈ
жғіе•ҸдёӢе…¶еҜҰжғіе…ҘиЎҢзҡ„и©ұпјҢдҝӮе’Әеҫ—з•ӘеҺ»еӨ–еңӢеҖӢй–ҖеӯёжҸёж©ҹе…Ҳпјҹ

е””и©Іе·ҙжү“


еҺ»еӨ–еңӢи©ҰдёҖи©Ұе…ҲжңғеҘҪе°‘е°‘
е””дҝӮдәәдәәйғҪж„ӣ/еӨ©з”ҹйҒ©еҗҲйЈӣ
еҒҮеҰӮи©Ұе®ҢзҷјзҸҫиҮӘе·ұеҘҪйҗҳж„ҸйЈӣпјҢиҖҢдё”theoryеҸҲи®Җеҫ—жҺӮеҳ…и©ұпјҢз•¶з„¶дҝӮжңҖеҘҪ

еҰӮжһңеҸӘеҸҜз•ҷйҰҷжёҜпјҢе’Ғе”Ҝжңүи®Җе·Ұtheoryе…ҲпјҢд№ӢеҫҢи«—ж–№жі•пјҲдҫӢеҰӮcathay pacific cadet program(?))
и®Җе·Ұз„ЎеЈһ дҝӮи®Җеҫ—жҺӮеҳ…и©ұеҜҰжңүдёҠдҪҚж©ҹжңғпјҢдёҚйҒҺеҸҜиғҪжңғж…ўе°‘е°‘

жҲ‘е””жғіе’Ғи¬ӣпјҢдҪҶз„ЎеҸҜеҗҰиӘҚжңүйҢўзңҹдҝӮжңғеҸҜд»Ҙеҝ«еҘҪеӨҡ...
дҪҶж…ўе””д»ЈиЎЁе…Ҙе””еҲ°иЎҢпјҢе‘ўй»һиҰҒиЁҳдҪҸ

Q8.
One cause of high oil temperature and low oil pressure could be

a. Very low oil level in the sump
b. The oil's viscosity is too high for the engine type
c. The oil sump is overfilled
d. The oil cooler bypass is not working


A
Too few oil for cooling: high temp
Low quantity of oil: low oil pressure

Q11

As the altitude increases, with the same power and mixture setting, how will the power output be affected and why?


Higher power output
Higher alt: air less dense, less air will be available for mixture
Fuel to air ratio: increase
Richer mixture: higher power

Q13.

a. Increase throttle before manifold pressure
b. Increase manifold pressure before increasing throttle
c. Increase throttle and decrease manifold pressure slightly
d. Increase manifold pressure slightly, then apply full throttle, then apply full manifold pressure

Q12.

If an aircraft fitted with constant speed unit(CSU) has an increase in RPM, how will this affect the propeller torque?


Engine with CSU: Variable pitch propller
Increase in RPM: finer pitch config
Finer pitch: more thrust and higher torque

Since oil is useful for the engine, it is important to let pilot knowing how exactly is the oil doing in the engine

Q11

As the altitude increases, with the same power and mixture setting, how will the power output be affected and why?


Higher power output
Higher alt: air less dense, less air will be available for mixture
Fuel to air ratio: increase
Richer mixture: higher power


Ans: lower power output

As the altitude increases, the air is less dense. You will end up having less air particles for the combustion, and also an over-rich mixture setting

Q12.

If an aircraft fitted with constant speed unit(CSU) has an increase in RPM, how will this affect the propeller torque?


Engine with CSU: Variable pitch propller
Increase in RPM: finer pitch config
Finer pitch: more thrust and higher torque


For an aircraft fitted with CSU, when the RPM is sensed to be increasing, in order to maintain the original RPM, the aircraft propeller will be changed towards a coarse pitch(increase blade angle)

This will increase the propeller torque, which is a force to avoid the increase in propeller movement

Richer mixture: higher power


Ans: lower power output

As the altitude increases, the air is less dense. You will end up having less air particles for the combustion, and also an over-rich mixture setting

Q14.
For aircraft with a CSU, in order to increase the power without overboosting the engine, you should

a. Increase throttle before manifold pressure
b. Increase manifold pressure before increasing throttle
c. Increase throttle and decrease manifold pressure slightly
d. Increase manifold pressure slightly, then apply full throttle, then apply full manifold pressure
Lm
